jokes.text: Can't believe Michael Bay spent a billion dollars of his own money to build that for The Rock.
jokes.setup: Andy sees Alcatraz prison in San Francisco
jokes.punchline: He thinks Michael Bay personally built the real prison as a movie set
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - earnestly believing something completely absurd about Hollywood spending. Character-specific stupidity that feels genuine.

jokes.text: I can't believe you were able to recite that entire movie, from memory, on the plane ride.
jokes.setup: Leslie responds to Andy's Alcatraz comment
jokes.punchline: Reveals Andy quoted the entire Rock movie during their flight
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation of Andy's movie obsession. Leslie's exasperated tone sells the absurdity of sitting through that.

jokes.text: What happens at a National Parks Conference is compiled and emailed to you in a PDF the following Monday.
jokes.setup: Leslie starts with 'You know what they say' implying a catchy Vegas-style phrase
jokes.punchline: Delivers the most bureaucratic, unsexy conclusion possible
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of 'What happens in Vegas...' with peak Leslie bureaucratic enthusiasm. Perfect structure and character voice.

jokes.text: They give tours. Yeah. That's exactly how they'll be expecting me to try to break in.
jokes.setup: Andy wants to break into Alcatraz, Ben suggests tours
jokes.punchline: Andy treats the obvious legal option as too obvious for his master plan
jokes.explanation: Andy's conspiracy-level thinking applied to tourist attractions. His serious delivery of illogical reasoning is very in-character.

jokes.text: Wow. I've never worked hard on anything. What a cool life!
jokes.setup: Tom reflects on this being the hardest he's worked since...
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's never worked hard and celebrates this fact
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom moment - accidentally revealing his shallow work ethic then immediately celebrating it as a positive. Great self-awareness.

jokes.text: From now on, everyone call me Kristen. Because I am wigging out right now!
jokes.setup: Tom panics about the rushed restaurant opening
jokes.punchline: Makes a terrible wig/wigging out pun while having a breakdown
jokes.explanation: Weak pun but Tom's tendency to make bad wordplay during stress is character-appropriate. The desperation sells it slightly.

jokes.text: They're not even done painting that sign. It says 'Tom's bi.'
jokes.setup: Tom lists reasons they're not ready to open
jokes.punchline: The sign is incomplete in an unfortunately suggestive way
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag that works through implication. Not groundbreaking but efficiently executed for the panic situation.

jokes.text: Lester Kanopf? Come on, guys, get it together.
jokes.setup: Someone calls Grant by the wrong name
jokes.punchline: Grant's mild exasperation at the mix-up
jokes.explanation: Minor character beat. Shows government bureaucracy inefficiency but not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: Liam Bonneville? The Department of the Interior's resident bad boy?
jokes.setup: Grant mentions Liam Bonneville will be at lunch
jokes.punchline: Leslie calls a parks bureaucrat a 'bad boy'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - genuinely excited about the most boring possible 'bad boy.' Great character voice and worldview.

jokes.text: No idea.
jokes.setup: Ben explains they're pitching to Gryzzl, asked what Gryzzl does
jokes.punchline: Admits complete ignorance about the company they're pitching
jokes.explanation: Ben's blunt honesty about being unprepared. Two words that perfectly capture government inefficiency and his character.

jokes.text: Six months ago, I was working in a taco truck. Now, I own one-third of the Portland Trailblazers.
jokes.setup: Employee describes Gryzzl's rapid success
jokes.punchline: Extreme leap from taco truck to NBA ownership
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing tech company excess. The specificity of Portland Trailblazers makes it feel real and absurd.

jokes.text: Sure, obviously, we're no Akron. I mean, we're more like Dayton. But with your help, we can become Toledo.
jokes.setup: Gryzzl mentions focusing on bigger cities like Akron
jokes.punchline: Ben's increasingly modest comparison cities showing Pawnee's lowly status
jokes.explanation: Great escalating desperation. Ben naming increasingly unglamorous mid-size cities as aspirational goals is perfectly pathetic.

jokes.text: We don't like to say the word 'no'... No. ...at this company. We feel it's counter-productive to our creative mission. So instead, let me say, 'Nah, bro.'
jokes.setup: Employee explains they don't say 'no' as it's counter-productive
jokes.punchline: Immediately says 'no' then offers 'nah, bro' as if it's different
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of corporate doublespeak. The immediate contradiction followed by the meaningless alternative is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: Check it out, guys, all done. Only made, like, 12 mistakes.
jokes.setup: Andy finishes cutting someone's hair at Gryzzl
jokes.punchline: Proudly announces he made 12 mistakes
jokes.explanation: Andy's proud incompetence. Treating 12 mistakes as a reasonable number for a haircut is wonderfully absurd.

jokes.text: Dwayne 'The Rock' Swanson.
jokes.setup: Tom greets Ron
jokes.punchline: Combines Ron's name with The Rock
jokes.explanation: Simple name pun that fits Tom's style but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: Who do you think I am, Thomas Hucker?
jokes.setup: Tom asks for chairs immediately, Ron says his chairs take time
jokes.punchline: Ron references an obscure furniture maker as if everyone knows him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - dropping woodworking references like they're common knowledge. Shows his deep expertise and social obliviousness.

jokes.text: Please, Ron? I've never asked you for anything today.
jokes.setup: Tom begs Ron for the chairs
jokes.punchline: Claims he's never asked for anything 'today' instead of 'ever'
jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay that works because it shows Tom's desperation and tendency toward meaningless qualifiers.

jokes.text: I made a poster of you by blowing up your picture from the National Parks newsletter. I cut out your face and made it look like you were dunking over Charles Barkley.
jokes.setup: Leslie mentions having Rebecca's poster on her wall as a kid
jokes.punchline: Reveals the elaborate homemade poster creation process
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Leslie's bureaucratic hero worship. The specificity of Charles Barkley makes it perfectly absurd and character-driven.

jokes.text: Rebecca Varuvian. Yes? Nothing. That was the best lunch I ever had.
jokes.setup: Leslie calls out to Rebecca
jokes.punchline: Has nothing to say, just wanted to express joy about lunch
jokes.explanation: Leslie's awkward fangirling. The anti-climax of having nothing to say after calling someone's name is endearingly awkward.

jokes.text: I agree with you on all things. Throughout history. Until the end of time, forever.
jokes.setup: Michelle Obama asks if Leslie agrees with her
jokes.punchline: Leslie's increasingly extreme declarations of agreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie starstruck escalation. Each addition makes her more ridiculously sycophantic in a believable way.

jokes.text: Great work! I'm sorry I'm talking so loud! I'm getting louder and now I can't stop! My apologies!
jokes.setup: Leslie gets excited talking to Michelle Obama
jokes.punchline: Her volume control completely fails and she acknowledges it while still yelling
jokes.explanation: Great escalating physical comedy through dialogue. Leslie's self-awareness while being unable to stop makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I thought he was gonna be on it.
jokes.setup: Andy and April watch a shopping cart go down a San Francisco hill
jokes.punchline: Andy expected to ride the cart but just pushed it empty
jokes.explanation: April's deadpan disappointment at Andy's anticlimactic stunt. The expectation subversion works through her character.

jokes.text: I don't know! Liam Bonneville has me in a tizzy!
jokes.setup: Ben asks why she didn't lead with meeting Michelle Obama
jokes.punchline: Leslie is more excited about the parks bureaucrat than the First Lady
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to the 'bad boy' joke. Leslie's priorities being completely backwards is quintessential character comedy.

jokes.text: No. Can't I just stay in the back and spit in people's food like we planned?
jokes.setup: Tom asks April to handle service at the restaurant
jokes.punchline: April preferred their original plan of food contamination
jokes.explanation: Perfect April misanthropy. The casual mention of spitting in food as if it were a reasonable business plan.

jokes.text: Fine, but if he raises his voice once, once, I'm out. Please don't stick to that policy.
jokes.setup: Donna sets strict conditions about Craig's yelling
jokes.punchline: Tom immediately begs her not to follow through
jokes.explanation: Good character interplay. Tom's immediate panic about losing Donna shows his desperation and knowledge of Craig's inevitable outbursts.

jokes.text: What are these pictures? Oh, geez, I must have given the printer the wrong flash drive. What is this, a rotten grapefruit? No, it's my dog's rectum.
jokes.setup: Tom discovers wrong pictures on the menus
jokes.punchline: Larry's dog's medical photos instead of food photos
jokes.explanation: Brilliant gross-out escalation. The visual of dog rectum photos on restaurant menus is perfectly horrible and Larry-specific.

jokes.text: Larry? Larry. Larry. Listen to me. This is the worst thing you've ever done.
jokes.setup: Tom processes the menu disaster
jokes.punchline: Declares it Larry's worst mistake ever with building intensity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Tom's horror. The repetition of Larry's name building to the pronouncement works well dramatically.

jokes.text: It was too perfect. It looked machine-made.
jokes.setup: Tom asks what was wrong with a chair Ron just destroyed
jokes.punchline: Ron's impossibly high standards for handmade furniture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron craftsmanship obsession. Destroying something for being too good is exactly his impossible standards.

jokes.text: Every time someone in Pawnee clicks through a slideshow of American Music Award red carpet sideboob fails, they'll say, 'Thank you, Ben Wyatt.'
jokes.setup: Ben wants to give Pawnee free WiFi as a going-away present
jokes.punchline: His noble gesture reduced to enabling clickbait consumption
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deflation of Ben's noble intentions. The specificity of 'sideboob fails' makes it perfectly internet-accurate and pathetic.

jokes.text: There can't be an alchemist of the Hinterlands. The Hinterlands is a shadow kingdom that can only sustain a provost or a denier.
jokes.setup: Gryzzl employee jokes about being an alchemist character
jokes.punchline: Ben immediately corrects the game lore with intense specificity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben nerdiness. His immediate, serious correction of fictional game lore shows his obsessive attention to detail.

jokes.text: You lost your cityhood?
jokes.setup: Government merger expert mentions his town became unincorporated territory
jokes.punchline: Leslie's horrified realization of what she's risking
jokes.explanation: Leslie's horror at the ultimate government failure. The phrase 'lost your cityhood' treated like a terminal diagnosis is funny.

jokes.text: Which is it? Fruity or earthy? It can't be both, they're mutually exclusive!
jokes.setup: Donna describes wine as fruity and earthy
jokes.punchline: Craig has a meltdown over contradictory flavor descriptors
jokes.explanation: Craig's perfectionist anxiety manifesting over wine descriptions. His overreaction to minor inconsistency is very in-character.

jokes.text: Which is it? Fruity or earthy? It can't be both, they're mutually exclusive!
jokes.setup: Donna describes wine as fruity and earthy
jokes.punchline: Craig's wine snob meltdown over contradiction
jokes.explanation: Classic Craig intensity - his wine expertise creates impossible standards

jokes.text: A man without a palate isn't a man. I love you, Donna.
jokes.setup: Craig continues his wine tasting meltdown
jokes.punchline: Declares his masculinity depends on taste buds, then professes love to Donna
jokes.explanation: Craig's dramatic personality in full display. The immediate swing from existential crisis to love declaration is perfectly manic.

jokes.text: Guess I have to start over. Who wants to go look for trees with me?
jokes.setup: Ron breaks another chair after Tom breaks a glass
jokes.punchline: Casually suggests starting the entire woodworking process over
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron response to superstition. His willingness to start completely over from finding trees shows his commitment to ritual.

jokes.text: I move my abbot to the ocean hex, which moves my brinksman to the devil's lair, and pushes my farmer, yes, my humble farmer, directly into the central cone.
jokes.setup: Ben makes his final move in Cones of Dunshire
jokes.punchline: Delivers an impossibly complex game-winning move with building drama
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff to Ben's game obsession. The ridiculous complexity and his dramatic delivery of 'humble farmer' makes it epic and absurd.

jokes.text: Yeah, man! We did it! This whole company is ours! Oh, no, it's not. That's mine. That's mine. It's all mine.
jokes.setup: Andy celebrates their Cones victory
jokes.punchline: Ben corrects Andy about who actually owns what
jokes.explanation: Good partnership dynamic joke about ownership confusion

jokes.text: Spaghetti, linguine, fettuccine, ravioli, rigatoni, Ferrari, Lamborghini, bucatini, lasagna.
jokes.setup: April lists pasta dishes from memory for customers
jokes.punchline: Sneaks Italian car brands into the pasta list
jokes.explanation: Clever April sabotage disguised as helpfulness. The cars blend seamlessly into the pasta names, showing her subtle troublemaking.

jokes.text: We had to throw our menus away because they are covered with pictures of Larry's dog's rectum.
jokes.setup: Customer asks for a menu
jokes.punchline: April matter-of-factly explains the dog rectum photo situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect April deadpan delivery of the most inappropriate restaurant explanation possible. Her casual tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Great news. Lord Sheldon's gonna make it. Yeah. We just have to apply a salve to his anus every hour for the rest of his life, but... Oh! Enjoy your meal!
jokes.setup: Larry shares good news about his dog's health
jokes.punchline: Provides graphic medical details to restaurant customers
jokes.explanation: Larry's complete social obliviousness on full display. Graphic medical details followed by 'enjoy your meal' is perfectly inappropriate.

jokes.text: I mean, people are playing with my cones, babe.
jokes.setup: Ben explains his game has spread across the internet
jokes.punchline: Unintentionally suggestive phrasing about his game pieces
jokes.explanation: Mild innuendo that works because Ben is completely oblivious to how it sounds. Low-hanging fruit but fits the character.

jokes.text: Wasn't Dolly the sheep like 20 years ago? It's like, 'What the hell, scientists? Get it together!'
jokes.setup: Leslie wishes for human cloning to solve her dilemma
jokes.punchline: Blames scientists for not advancing cloning fast enough
jokes.explanation: Leslie's impatient government worker attitude applied to scientific progress. Her expectation that cloning should be routine by now is amusing.

jokes.text: I love quitting. When I was a kid and things didn't go my way, I would just take my ball and go home. That's better than winning, 'cause then, your friends can't play anymore!
jokes.setup: Ron talks about finishing jobs you start
jokes.punchline: April celebrates quitting and ruining things for others
jokes.explanation: Perfect April misanthropy. Finding joy in ruining others' fun rather than winning is exactly her twisted logic.

jokes.text: Well, I hate doing work, but I love being flattered, so maybe I'll give it another try.
jokes.setup: April encourages Tom not to quit the restaurant
jokes.punchline: Tom's honest assessment of his motivations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom honesty about his shallow motivations. The balance between laziness and ego is spot-on for his character.

jokes.text: Because you currently owe me $16,000. For the wood. The labor was free, but the wood was a bargain. At $16,000.
jokes.setup: Ron explains why Tom should keep trying with the restaurant
jokes.punchline: Reveals massive wood costs, calling it a 'bargain'
jokes.explanation: Great Ron moment about his expensive taste in materials. Calling $16,000 worth of wood a 'bargain' is perfectly Ron.

jokes.text: Wait. I'm on Endor. These are the redwoods George Lucas used to create the forest moon of Endor.
jokes.setup: Ben and Leslie are in the California redwoods
jokes.punchline: Ben immediately identifies the Star Wars filming location
jokes.explanation: Ben's nerd knowledge interrupting a romantic moment. His priorities being pop culture over nature is very characteristic.

jokes.text: Might be for the best. But anyway, I love you. No, I don't. I'm sorry. That was weird. Okay, bye.
jokes.setup: Leslie accepts the job over the phone while emotional
jokes.punchline: Accidentally tells Grant she loves him, then awkwardly retracts it
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment as Leslie's emotions spill over inappropriately. The immediate backtracking makes it more awkward.

jokes.text: She wanted to fire me, but she realized that she loved having me around too much, and she couldn't do it.
jokes.setup: Larry explains why Leslie looked emotional after their conversation
jokes.punchline: Completely misinterprets Leslie's job announcement as love for him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry obliviousness. His complete misreading of the situation while being somewhat touched is endearingly pathetic.

jokes.text: Did someone call for a party zoo?
jokes.setup: Jean-Ralphio arrives to help with the restaurant
jokes.punchline: Plays on 'party animal' but makes it sound like an actual zoo
jokes.explanation: Typical Jean-Ralphio wordplay entrance line

jokes.text: All right, so we got a four-way going with my ex, my brother, and this rando? Okay, if that's where the night is going. It's 9:00 in the morning.
jokes.setup: Tom introduces Jean-Ralphio and Mona Lisa to Craig
jokes.punchline: Mona Lisa immediately misinterprets the business meeting sexually
jokes.explanation: In-character Mona Lisa inappropriateness. Jean-Ralphio's time correction adds to the absurdity of her assumption.

jokes.text: You remember the time you made me dress up as you, and go on a date with one of your ex-boyfriends? And then the guy immediately figured it out, and beat me up?
jokes.setup: Mona Lisa explains why Jean-Ralphio owes her a favor
jokes.punchline: Reveals increasingly absurd past scheme that ended in violence
jokes.explanation: Great escalating backstory that reveals the twins' chaotic relationship. Each detail makes it worse and more believable for them.

jokes.text: I wanna go horseback riding! Deal.
jokes.setup: Craig suggests go-karts with Jean-Ralphio
jokes.punchline: Mona Lisa randomly suggests horseback riding, Craig immediately agrees
jokes.explanation: Random absurdity that works through the characters' manic energy. Craig's immediate agreement shows his impulsive nature.

jokes.text: You're right, the name is too long, I was picturing bigger hats.
jokes.setup: Andy comments on the long name of their volunteer group
jokes.punchline: Leslie's solution was bigger hats rather than shorter names
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie logic - solve practical problems with more elaborate solutions. Bigger hats for longer names is wonderfully absurd.

jokes.text: Only a moron would ever live anywhere other than Pawnee, Indiana. Leslie Knope, multiple occasions.
jokes.setup: Ron reads Leslie's quote on her statue as she's about to leave town
jokes.punchline: The quote is hilariously ironic given her decision to move
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic timing. The statue quote becoming immediately ironic as Leslie leaves is perfectly structured dramatic irony.

jokes.text: I ordered a small cheeseburger and both the buns were pizzas.
jokes.setup: Musician comments on Pawnee being an amazing town
jokes.punchline: Describes the most Pawnee food innovation possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pawnee food absurdity. Pizza-bun cheeseburgers capture the town's bizarre culinary excess in one perfect image.

jokes.text: Judge gave me two ankle bracelets. Said it was the first time he ever had to do that. Ja boy's a question on the bar exam! Ya heard!
jokes.setup: Jean-Ralphio mentions house arrest
jokes.punchline: Proudly claims his case is so unique it's legally educational
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jean-Ralphio pride in being terrible - turning legal trouble into bragging rights

jokes.text: Ja boy's a question on the bar exam! Ya heard!
jokes.setup: Jean-Ralphio mentions having two ankle bracelets
jokes.punchline: Claims his legal troubles are now used to test lawyers
jokes.explanation: Peak Jean-Ralphio bragging about his criminal incompetence. Turning legal trouble into a point of pride is very in-character.

jokes.text: Unless you want me to get on stage and talk about how we used to take baths together. How you cried whenever I took away your rubber duckies. Damn, Donna, why you gotta bring the Quackson Five into this?
jokes.setup: Donna threatens to embarrass Ginuwine if he doesn't come to Tom's restaurant
jokes.punchline: Reveals intimate childhood details and his name for his rubber ducks
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation ending with 'Quackson Five' - a perfect pun that reveals the depth of their shared history and Ginuwine's childhood.

jokes.text: Save it, jagwipe. I had this place on lockdown. I was rolling in cash and lonely moms, and then you come out of nowhere, and you blow up my spot.
jokes.setup: Freddy confronts Andy about children's entertainment
jokes.punchline: Treats kids' parties like competitive business territory
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of children's entertainer rivalry into street-level competition

jokes.text: No, just trolling for some daddy. Sorry, I pronounced that wrong. I'm trolling for some dad-dy.
jokes.setup: Ron asks what brings Tammy to a children's event
jokes.punchline: Tammy makes innocent word sound sexual through emphasis
jokes.explanation: Classic Tammy inappropriate sexualization. The fake correction that makes it worse is very her manipulative style.

jokes.text: I will defeat you right into my pants.
jokes.setup: Tammy threatens Ron that she will defeat him
jokes.punchline: Clarifies that defeat means seduction
jokes.explanation: Peak Tammy predatory behavior. Her direct, crude approach to seduction is consistently in-character and disturbing.

jokes.text: There's pizza in Pawnee. Right. Thanks, babe. You're so good at reminding me where pizza is.
jokes.setup: Andy argues for moving to Chicago because of pizza
jokes.punchline: April sarcastically thanks him for the obvious information
jokes.explanation: Perfect April sarcasm about Andy's simple logic. Her mock gratitude for his pizza geography lesson is spot-on.

jokes.text: I like to do nothing with no one. So, thank you and I love you, but no thank you, and I hate you. More I love you.
jokes.setup: April explains why she doesn't want to move to Chicago
jokes.punchline: Contradictory emotional declarations that somehow make sense
jokes.explanation: Brilliant April emotional expression. Her contradictory feelings delivered with perfect logic shows her complex relationship with affection.

jokes.text: What? Larry's great. Have you lost your mind?
jokes.setup: Leslie horrified that only Larry wants to come to Chicago
jokes.punchline: Ben genuinely thinks Larry would be great to bring
jokes.explanation: Ben's contrarian appreciation for Larry versus everyone else's horror. His genuine confusion at Leslie's reaction is endearing.

jokes.text: I wonder if the carpet is as curly as the drapes.
jokes.setup: Tammy notices Jeremy Jamm's curly hair
jokes.punchline: Makes crude speculation about his body hair
jokes.explanation: Standard crude double entendre that works because it's exactly what Tammy would say. Not particularly clever but fits character.

jokes.text: Let's get divorced. That way we can get married again! Yes! Let's do it. We should do that, right? Yeah, totally. We're getting divorced! Yes! This is amazing!
jokes.setup: Andy wishes he could marry April again
jokes.punchline: They decide to divorce specifically to remarry
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd Andy and April logic. Their enthusiasm for divorce as a means to more marriage is perfectly them.

jokes.text: And I've been to Dallas.
jokes.setup: Dr. Saperstein praises Tom's Italian food
jokes.punchline: Claims Dallas as his Italian food authority
jokes.explanation: Great understated absurdity. Dallas being presented as the pinnacle of Italian cuisine authority is perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: And by the way, don't think that we are not discussing Duke Silver. When were you going to tell me about that? Unbelievable. I am so furious at you. But I've already forgiven you and you need to teach me how to play the saxophone.
jokes.setup: Leslie processes Ron's secret saxophone performance
jokes.punchline: Rapid emotional journey from fury to forgiveness to demands
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie emotional processing speed. Her ability to be angry, forgive, and make demands in one breath is very characteristic.

jokes.text: And if you don't agree with us, it's nine against one and we can make you disappear forever. What? I said we have free WiFi.
jokes.setup: Andy threatens Grant about Leslie staying in Pawnee
jokes.punchline: Covers the threat with innocent WiFi comment
jokes.explanation: Good Andy intimidation attempt with innocent cover. His ability to sound threatening then immediately innocent is amusing.

jokes.text: Also, can you bring back Power Rangers? I don't know what it is you do, but you seem important enough to get that done.
jokes.setup: Andy addresses Grant about various requests
jokes.punchline: Assumes government official can resurrect TV shows
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy misunderstanding of government power. His assumption that any official can bring back Power Rangers is endearingly naive.
